title: Life threatening bleeding pressure routine
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for direct pressure, clean covering, and urgent escalation for severe bleeding. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize direct pressure, clean covering, and urgent escalation for severe bleeding. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: spurting blood, soaking cloths, amputation, deep wound, dizziness, or shock signs. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «Life threatening bleeding pressure routine»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «Life threatening bleeding pressure routine»
paper and pen. Context: “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”.
phone or radio if available — for «Life threatening bleeding pressure routine»
labels for times and medicines — for «Life threatening bleeding pressure routine»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.

Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.

Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”.
Escalate urgently if you see: spurting blood, soaking cloths, amputation, deep wound, dizziness, or shock signs.

Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: spurting blood, soaking cloths, amputation, deep wound, dizziness, or shock signs
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “Life threatening bleeding pressure routine” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to Life threatening bleeding pressure routine.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to Life threatening bleeding pressure routine.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to Life threatening bleeding pressure routine.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to Life threatening bleeding pressure routine.

title: Shock warmth and monitoring routine
